title: Radiation anomaly characterization system
abstract: A system for detecting radiation events, having: a test memory with memory blocks; a controller having controller memory; an integrated circuit (IC) array having IC chains, wherein each of the IC chains has a plurality of IC elements that is response to and generates a voltage when exposed to a radiation event; a dosimeter configured to record an accumulated amount of radiation exposure to the system, wherein the controller is configured to perform steps of: initializing the test memory, the IC array and the dosimeter; monitoring for an unexpected signal from an IC chain and for unexpected data in a memory block of the test memory; and identifying the radiation event upon one or more of receiving the unexpected signal and identifying unexpected data in a memory block of the test memory.
claims: 1. A system for detecting radiation events, comprising: a test memory having memory blocks; a controller having controller memory; an integrated circuit (IC) array having IC chains, wherein each of the IC chains has a plurality of IC elements that is response to and generates a voltage when exposed to a radiation event; a dosimeter configured to record an accumulated amount of radiation exposure to the system, wherein the controller is configured to perform steps of: initializing the test memory, the IC array and the dosimeter; monitoring for an unexpected signal from an IC chain and for unexpected data in a memory block of the test memory; and identifying the radiation event upon one or more of receiving the unexpected signal and identifying unexpected data in a memory block of the test memory.
